caption:
This is my new favorite AI prompt for creative strategy:

(And it’s SUPER beginner friendly)

In order to scale on Meta Ads in 2026, we know that you need to advertise to your different personas or audience segments.

But no one ever talks about how to actually find those personas… and distribute those learnings across your teams.

With @claudeai’s new @meetgamma connector, I’m able to brainstorm customer personas for the brands I work with AND create full presentations without leaving Claude.

No more hopping between platforms. No more context switching. Just easier, quicker, and more effective brainstorming.

What used to take me ~2+ hours to make a deck now takes me… 2 minutes tops. Wild.

I’ll post the prompt I’m using in the pinned comment :) 

NGL… as someone who pretty much makes presentations for a living, this blew my mind.

Have you started making presentations like this yet?

#sponsored #gammapartner
firstComment: Gamma and Claude are like fire

latestComments.text: Gamma and Claude are like fire
AI for creative research is a multiplier. But knowing which insight is worth scaling still needs the strategist. That is the job.
Two hours to two minutes is the kind of efficiency that changes what's possible in a day. The connector game is getting ridiculous in the best way 🔥
Hi Dara can you please message me
The persona research step is where most brands skip and it shows in their creative. AI doesn't replace the strategist — it compresses the research loop so you test sharper angles faster.

replies.text: @itsryanto thank you!!

latestComments.text: Persona-level creative is the unlock most brands skip. One product, five buyer identities, five completely different hooks. That's how you scale without creative fatigue killing delivery.

The persona extraction from reviews is the real unlock here. Most brands skip this step entirely. They run the same broad targeting with generic creative and wonder why performance drops. When you pull verbatim language from customer reviews and build personas around triggers, fears, and desires, your ad angles practically write themselves. The creative stops being guesswork and starts being a direct response to what people already said they wanted. Smart workflow.

caption:
Meta just dropped some huge updates at Newfronts… let’s talk about it. 

1. GenAI is expanding on Ads Manager to include AI UGC AVATARS. This is meant to help advertisers that have 1000s of skus and can’t make ads for each one. 

2. Reels Trending Ads are expanding into cultural moments like F1 and NYFW so that brands can show up alongside of big events. 

I also got to speak on stage this year alongside @jennalyonsnyc and @carlradke. HUGE. 

What do you think?? 

#metapartner
firstComment: The AI UGC avatars are going to be a game changer for catalog-heavy brands. We've seen DTC brands with 500+ SKUs spend 70% of their creative budget just trying to cover product lines. If this actually works at quality, it could flip the whole creative production model. The trending ads piece tied to cultural moments is smart too — we've tested event-based hooks and they consistently outperform evergreen by 2-3x on CTR.

caption:
Dear Tiffany from Brand Marketing:

The role of a creative strategist is NOT to:
 • Research competitor ads
 • Write endless briefs
 • Manage the creative pipeline
 • Feed the content calendar

Your job as a creative strategist IS to grow the business.

Which means you need to ruthlessly prioritize based on leverage:

✅ High leverage: Evergreen creative that taps into new audiences and scales for months

❌ Low leverage: 20 assets for a 1 week Valentine’s push that gets paused immediately

Look, I’m not saying you should completely ignore brand marketing when they need calendar assets...

But you should have an MVP process to handle those requests quickly so you can focus on what actually matters: finding the creative that brings in new customers at scale. (Ones that don’t have a finite timeline)

If you’re spending 75% of your time on short-term campaigns, you’re not doing creative strategy. 

You’re doing marketing coordination.

caption:
How I’m wielding this new power:

- getting a gut check on what’s actually getting eyeballs from brands
- breaking down catalog ads vs influencer/celeb vs other formats 
- seeing what messaging / persona / product trends are rising to the top
- petty stuff like: did this campaign actually work?

What am I missing??⁠

Granola.ai. Meeting note taker that summarizes and lets you chat with the meeting. Also lets you chat with all meetings to gain insight. Game changer. Great sharing features for teams.

caption:
Plot twist: Your streaming preferences might be a relationship dealbreaker 💔

I just got back from @tubi’s Stream 2026 launch event, and the data is wild:

1/3 of audiences will break up with their partner because of misaligned streaming tastes.

As someone who takes their “streaming time sacred” very seriously... I actually get it.

But here’s what caught my attention as a marketer:

→ 75% say streaming together is quality time
→ 67% are more likely to support their fandoms through ads
→ Gen Z is open to MORE ads if it means free streaming or supporting creators

The advertising landscape is shifting fast. 

Audiences aren’t just tolerating ads… they’re willing to engage with them when there’s clear value exchange.

This is huge for how we think about creative strategy in 2026.

Download The Stream 2026 in my link in bio.

#tubipartner
